runs.reply:
A guy is sitting at home when he hears a knock at the door. He opens it and sees a snail on the porch. He picks it up and throws it as far as he can into the yard.

Three years later, there’s another knock at the door. He opens it, and there’s the same snail. 

The snail looks up at him and says, "What the heck was that all about?"

A guy is walking late at night when he sees a sign in a yard that says: **"Talking Dog for Sale."**

Intrigued, he rings the doorbell. The owner comes to the door and tells him the dog is in the backyard. 

The guy goes into the backyard and sees a black lab sitting there. "You talk?" he asks.

"Yep," the lab replies.

The guy is stunned. "So, what's your story?"

The lab looks up and says, "Well, I discovered my gift when I was a puppy. I wanted to help the government, so I told the CIA. In no time, they had me jetting from country to country, sitting in rooms with spies and world leaders, because no one suspected a dog would be eavesdropping. I was their most valuable spy for eight years. 

But the travel tired me out, so I settled down and took a job at the airport doing undercover security—wandering near suspicious characters and listening in. I uncovered some major smuggling rings and was awarded a load of medals. I got married, had a mess of puppies, and now I'm just retired."

The guy is absolutely amazed. He goes back inside and asks the owner how much he wants for the dog.

"Ten dollars," the owner says.

"Ten dollars?!" the guy exclaims. "This dog is amazing! He's a war hero! A spy! Why on earth are you selling him so cheap?"

The owner rolls his eyes and says, "Because he’s a liar. He didn't do any of that."

Soft breeze through the trees,
Golden sun begins to set,
Day turns into night.

Golden sun descends,
Painting clouds in soft pink light,
Quiet night begins.

Painting shadows on the hills,
Daylight fades to rest.

The golden sun begins to set,
In shades of pink and violet.
The world grows still, the shadows creep,
As weary flowers fall asleep.

**Time is a river.**

It flows in one direction, never pauses for obstacles, and even when the surface looks still, there is an unstoppable current underneath moving you toward an unknown sea.

**Time is a river.**

It flows in one direction, never stopping for obstacles. You can dip your hand into the water, but the droplets that touch your skin are gone an instant later, replaced by a current that is constantly new. You can try to swim against it, but eventually, everyone is carried toward the same vast, unknown sea.

**"Hope is a walking stick in a dark forest."**

It doesn’t clear the path or turn on the lights, but it gives you a way to feel your way forward without falling.

The word is **Vellumine** (pronounced: *vel-yuh-meen*).

**Definition:** The soft, comforting glow of moonlight filtered through thin clouds or fog. 

**Example sentence:** "The forest was bathed in a soft *vellumine*, making the trees look like silver ghosts."

The word is **Vellumistry**.

**Definition:** The specific, nostalgic scent of old library books, dust, and decaying paper. 

**Used in a sentence:** "As she stepped into the basement archive, she was overwhelmed by the comforting sense of *vellumistry*."

The word is **Vexilluminescence**.

**Definition:** The soft, shimmering glow reflecting off a flag or banner when it catches the light of a setting sun.

**Used in a sentence:** "As the explorers reached the summit, the **vexilluminescence** of their tattered colors provided a brief moment of beauty against the darkening sky."
